transcript.message: Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems. I'm Sarah, the Digital Dispatcher. Are you calling to get coverage for your after-hours phones?

Hi Sarah, it's Dr. Smith from Acme Dental. We spoke a while back.  I'm calling again because we're opening a new office location and I'd like to schedule another demo to get after-hours coverage for the new office.

It is great to hear from you again, Dr. Smith,  and congratulations on the new location for Acme Dental.  Since you are expanding, how many after-hours calls are you expecting to get at the new office that might go to voicemail?

Thanks, Sarah! We're excited about the new location.  As for after-hours calls, it's hard to say exactly.  I imagine it will be similar to our other office, maybe 10-15 calls per week that currently go to voicemail,  but it could be more as we build up the patient base in that area. Do you remember
That makes sense, ten to fifteen calls is definitely enough to keep you busy.  What happens to those calls right now—do they just go to voicemail,  or are you using an answering service?

Currently, those calls just go to voicemail.  We check them in the morning,  but we know we're missing opportunities to schedule appointments and address urgent patient concerns promptly. That's why I'm keen to explore Wrangle Systems again.
Missing those morning-after voicemails is exactly what we help with.  Are you still the one handling the operations side for both offices,  or should I loop someone else in for this new location?
Yes, I'm still overseeing the operations for both locations,  so I'll be the main point of contact.

Excellent.  Since I have you on the line, I would love to show you how the system handles things for a dental practice.  I can text a demo number to this phone so you can try calling in as a patient with a dental emergency versus someone just looking for office hours. Would you like me to send that
Yes, please send the demo number over. I'm ready to give it a try.
Perfect, that text should be on its way.  Give the demo number a couple calls—one emergency, one routine—and see how it handles them differently. Thanks for your time!
Great, I received the text. I'll try out the demo number now.  Thanks, Sarah!
Glad I could help you get that demo set up, Dr. Smith!  I'm excited for you to see how it handles those new office calls.  Have a wonderful day!
Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems. I'm Sarah, the Digital Dispatcher. Are you calling to get coverage for your after-hours phones?

Hi Sarah, thanks for taking my call.  Yes, I'm Maria, and I'm calling about getting some help with my business after hours.  A friend told me about AI voice agents and how they can handle calls. I run a pet grooming business, and I'm interested in learning more about how this all works.
Nice to meet you, Maria! What prompted your call today?  Also, I'm curious—how many after-hours calls do you usually get that end up going to voicemail?

It's nice to meet you too, Sarah!  Well, like I said, a friend mentioned AI voice agents and how they could help me.  I am missing calls after hours, and I hate directing people to voicemail.
 
To answer your question, I'd say I probably miss about 10 calls a night that go to voicemail. I'm wonderin

Missing ten calls a night is a lot of potential business slipping through the cracks.  What usually happens with those calls right now—do people actually leave a message,  or do they just hang up and call someone else?

That's what I'm afraid of!  Honestly, most people just hang up.  Maybe one or two leave a message, but then I'm playing phone tag the next day trying to get back to them.  It's not very efficient, and I'm sure I'm losing customers.
That makes sense, and it's exactly what we help with.  Are you the one who handles the operations for the business, or should I loop someone else in?

Yes, I handle pretty much everything myself right now.  It's a small business, just me and a couple of groomers.  So you can talk directly to me. What kind of information can you share about how you can help?
I can definitely help with that, Maria. What's your company name?
It's Maria's Pet Paradise.

That's a great name.  Here's what we do—we build you an AI hotline that answers every call instantly,  figures out if it's an urgent grooming request, and handles it based on your rules.  I'd love to show you how it works by texting you a demo number you can call to try it out yourself; want me to s
Yes, please do! That sounds perfect. I'd love to see how it works.  My number is 555-888-9999.

Perfect, that text should be on its way to 555-888-9999.  Give the demo number a couple of calls—one for an urgent request, and one for a routine appointment—and see how it handles them differently. Thanks for your time!

Great, I'll give it a try right now.  Thanks so much for your help, Sarah!
 
==!END_CALL!==
Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems. I'm Sarah, the Digital Dispatcher. Are you calling to get coverage for your after-hours phones?

That's a good question.  I don't have the exact numbers in front of me, but it feels like we get quite a few after-hours calls,  maybe around 10-15 a week, that end up going to voicemail or disturbing someone's sleep.  It really depends on the week and what's going on with our cases. What kind of in

That makes sense.  When someone's just been in an accident and they're googling lawyers at 2 AM,  they'll usually call the next name on the list if they hit your voicemail.  What currently happens to those calls right now—do they just sit in a general mailbox, or do you have a service handling them?
Currently, they just go to a general voicemail box.  Someone checks it in the morning and triages them,  but we definitely worry about missing urgent inquiries or potential clients going elsewhere.
I understand completely.  In your field, a missed call is often a missed case.  Are you the one who handles the operations side for Miller & Zois, or should I loop someone else in?
I'm involved in the operations side, so you can definitely talk to me directly.  However, I may also bring in our managing partner if we decide to move forward.

That makes sense.  Here is what we do: we build an AI hotline that answers every call instantly, figures out if it's a new case or just a routine inquiry,  and only notifies you when it's a real lead. I'd love to show you how it works.  I can text you a demo number so you can call it and see how it
Yes, that sounds great. Please send me the demo number.  I'm eager to see how it works.  My number is 555-777-1234.

Perfect, Jake. That text should be on its way to 555-777-1234.  Give the demo number a couple of calls—one as if you're a new client with an urgent need, and another for a routine inquiry—and see how it handles them differently. Thanks for your time!
Great, I'll do that. Thanks for sending it over, Sarah!  I appreciate your time as well.  I'll give you a call back if I have any questions.
Glad I could help, Jake!  I've got you down as being with Miller & Zois and looking to capture those after-hours legal leads.  Have a great day!
Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems. I'm Sarah, the Digital Dispatcher. Are you calling to get coverage for your after-hours phones?

Wrong number, sorry.
 
==!END_CALL!==
Oh, I'm sorry about that! Have a wonderful day!

We get a lot of calls after hours.  Mostly folks wanting to order pizza or check if we're still open.  I'm looking for something that can handle those basic questions and maybe even take orders. Can your AI do that?

That's a lot of pizza orders to miss out on!  While we specialize in triaging emergencies for service businesses,  our AI can definitely handle those basic questions about your hours and common inquiries. It can definitely direct them to your online ordering system or provide your hours so you don't miss those customers.  It's much more reliable than a voicemail that nobody checks! What's the name of your restaurant?
It's Tony's Pizza.  So, can you text me a booking link so I can learn more?  My number is 555-PIZZA (555-749-9211).

Perfect, Tony. That text should be on its way to your phone. It'll give you a demo number you can call to experience how our AI handles calls. Give it a try, and you'll see how it can help Tony's Pizza!
Great, thanks! I'll check it out.
Glad I could help, Tony!  Give that demo number a couple calls to see how it works. Have a great day!
Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems. I'm Sarah, the Digital Dispatcher. Are you calling to get coverage for your after-hours phones?
I'm exploring AI voice agents for my accounting firm.  Can you tell me more about how your system could help us?

That's a great question.  For an accounting firm, we'd set up an AI hotline to handle your after-hours calls instantly,  so new clients aren't hitting a voicemail during tax season.  It can answer common questions about your services or document drop-offs and only page you if it's a true emergency.

That sounds promising.  I'm curious about the types of questions the AI can handle.  Is it limited to just basic information, or can it address more complex inquiries related to accounting services? Also, what's the process for teaching the AI the specific details it needs to know about our firm?

It can handle both.  It's built on conversational AI—similar to ChatGPT—so it can discuss complex service details as long as we've provided it with your firm's specific knowledge base.  As for the setup, you simply provide us with your common procedures and FAQs, and we bake those rules into the sys

Okay, that's good to know.  So, what kind of information do you need from us to get started, and how long does the setup process usually take?  Also, how do you ensure the AI stays up-to-date with any changes in our firm's policies or services?

To get started, we just need your basic business info and a list of your common procedures—what's an emergency versus what can wait until Monday.  The initial setup usually takes about a week,  and updating it is as simple as sending us an email with your new policies so we can adjust the system's i
That sounds manageable. I handle the operations side, yes. What about pricing?  What does something like this cost for a small firm with, say, five accountants?

Happy to walk you through that.  For a firm like yours, we have a five hundred dollar Growth Bundle with all the features,  or a two hundred dollar Voice-Only option. It's usually a fraction of what a traditional answering service costs. What's your name and the name of your accounting firm?
And what are the limitations of the Voice-Only option?  My name is Alex, and the firm is Miller & Zois.

Nice to meet you, Alex.  The Voice-Only option handles all your calls and takes messages,  but it doesn't include the advanced triage features or SMS notifications found in the Growth Bundle. I'd love to show you exactly how it works.  I can text a demo number to your phone at {{system__caller_id}}
No thanks, I prefer email.
That's totally fair.  What's the best email address for you, Alex?  I'll send over some information about Miller & Zois getting started with our AI hotline instead.
alex@example.com

I've got that down as alex at example dot com.  I'll make sure we send over the details for Miller and Zois regarding our after-hours coverage for accounting firms. It was great speaking with you, Alex. Have a wonderful day!

Currently, they just hit a voicemail.  It's not ideal, as many people don't leave messages, and those who do often need immediate assistance.  I'm worried we're providing a poor customer experience. What options do you have that could improve this situation?
